text:
Deep learning (DL) in medical image analysis has become a promising area in the ever-growing amount of medical data generated by hospitals around the clock, and the ability to analyze large amounts of data directly and in real time. The proposed paper intends to discuss the various Deep Learning architectures that can be utilised in the medical field. Hence, various approaches of Deep Learning are being demonstrated with examples in various applications in medical field, such as using architectures such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), use of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) units and hybrid models.

This research performs an exhaustively systematic literature review on Deep Learning in Medical Imaging and Informatics (MLMI) to find trends in the five technical domains introduced in our previous paper. These five technical domains and their corresponding evaluation metrics were precision, recall, F1 score, FPR, FNR, FPT, FNRT, FPRt, FNRT, FMeasure, computation time, and model size. For this part of the work, a comprehensive search was done to see which papers were highly cited. Search criteria yielded that a high number of papers were published around 2021. MLMI papers are heavily written in Python. Some applications are done on skin cancer detection, tumour segmentation, etc. More details of the challenges and limitations present in MLMI research are further explained in the next section. They were found to be related to high lack of data in certain biomedical imaging modalities, lack of interpretation in results, and many security related issues. This study acts as an essential foundation for future MLMI and PHI research, as it addresses the current challenges and gaps, such as multi-modal analysis and lack of development in the federated learning approach.
